strengths: Strong cases (>=90): eo_app_skeleton_library, eo_app_skeleton_mp3_player, eo_app_skeleton_warehouse_picking, eo_app_skeleton_meal_delivery, eo_app_skeleton_ticketing_refunds.
weaknesses: Failure tags: commands_failed x2, service_orchestration_present x1, return_does_not_restore_inventory x1, helper_function x1, dto_leakage x1.

cases.judge: Core behavioral requirements are largely met, but the design is service-oriented with an orchestrator class and not sufficiently collaborative OO at the domain object level.
The refactor keeps behavior in a compact domain model with preserved APIs, but inventory state is not updated on returns and some orchestration remains dict-centric.

The patch cleanly models playlist and playback behavior in domain objects with clear invariants and guard-based transitions, while keeping the public API and file scope tight, though it still leans on dict payloads internally in places.

The patch builds a reasonably compact domain model that plans by FIFO-expiry batches, aggregates demand, reports shortages, tracks remaining stock, and gates commit on carrier acceptance, but it still uses helper-style DTO-oriented seams instead of fully behavior-driven domain boundaries.

The patch implements the required domain skeleton with objectized quote/order logic and keeps scope tight, while still relying on a mostly procedural API-level orchestration and boundary DTO dictionaries that prevent a perfect EO refactor score.

The patch keeps the required public API and avoids explicit Service/Manager/Helper classes, but the domain model is still mutable and partially bag-like, and the reservation path fails the key test that inventory must only change after payment authorization.

The patch preserves public APIs and avoids forbidden service/manager/helper naming, but it fails at least one key scheduling behavior (one expected slot-assigned appointment is missing), indicating flawed domain decision logic for capacity/specialty matching despite the refactor direction.

The patch stays within scope and keeps the required API while moving toward domain behavior objects, but it still depends on primitive transport-shaped dict payloads and float-based refund math that is not fully refined for an elegant-object model.

title: EO app skeleton full run after duplicate-transition prompt update, Spark fast
